name: Chevron
description_short: Chevron Federal Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit credit union offering competitive savings accounts, auto loans, mortgages, and other financial products to eligible members.
description_long:
Chevron Federal Credit Union operates as a member-owned, not-for-profit financial institution regulated by the NCUA (National Credit Union Administration). Based in Houston, Texas, the institution serves members through both in-person locations and digital channels, as evidenced by their 2026 Annual Meeting scheduled at the Hyatt Regency Downtown Houston and their emphasis on virtual appointment options.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including savings accounts (Money Market Savings at 3.20% APY, Share Certificates at 3.75% APY for 12-month terms), auto loans (4.99% APR for new autos with 12-60 month terms), and mortgages (5.375% rate for jumbo 5/6 ARM 30-year variable at 6.185% APR). They provide IRAs, HSAs, youth savings accounts, and personal lending services with flexible terms and customizable loan packages. Members also have access to Zelle for peer-to-peer money transfers and video banking appointments for personalized service.

Chevron FCU distinguishes itself through multiple eligibility pathways for membership including employment, association membership, and residency requirements. They emphasize a people-first philosophy positioning themselves against traditional banks, offer scholarship programs ($1,000 David P. Smay Scholarship for high school seniors), and provide a referral program ($25 per referred member). The credit union actively promotes fraud awareness education and maintains dedicated security resources for member protection.

As a credit union, Chevron FCU offers competitive rates and member-centric service, but membership eligibility is restricted and may not be available to all consumers. The website does not provide detailed information about membership requirements, fee structures, or loan approval timelines. Existing members report satisfaction, but potential members should verify their eligibility before applying.
services: Money Market Savings accounts (3.20% APY for $25,000+ balances)
Auto loans (4.99% APR for new vehicles, 12-60 month terms)
Home mortgages and refinancing (5.375% rate available)
Personal loans with flexible terms and customizable packages
Video banking appointments and remote consultations
Online banking and digital account management
pros: Competitive savings rates: Money Market Savings at 3.20% APY on $25,000+ balances and Share Certificates at 3.75% APY for 12-month terms
Low auto loan rates: 4.99% APR for new auto loans with flexible 12-60 month terms
Multiple membership eligibility pathways including employment, association membership, and residency
Video banking appointments available for convenient remote service within 15 minutes
Peer-to-peer money transfer via Zelle integration
Member referral program offering $25 per referred member who joins
Scholarship program for high school seniors ($1,000 David P. Smay Scholarship)
cons: Membership eligibility restrictions mean not all consumers can join; specific eligibility criteria not clearly detailed on homepage
Limited information provided on website about membership fees, minimum account balances, or loan approval requirements
Routing number and phone number provided, but no clear fee disclosure or terms of service document linked from main navigation
Website does not display the credit union's asset size, member count, or detailed product comparison tools
No information about overdraft fees, account closure fees, or other common banking charges
best_for: Eligible employees, members, or residents seeking competitive savings rates and lower loan costs through a credit union
Auto buyers and home buyers looking for fixed-rate financing with flexible terms and personalized service
Savers wanting higher APY on money market and certificate accounts compared to traditional banks
Members seeking video banking and remote appointment options for convenience
diagnosis: Chevron Federal Credit Union is best for individuals eligible for membership who want competitive savings rates and loan terms in a member-owned structure. The primary caveat is that membership eligibility is restricted and must be verified before applying—not all consumers will qualify regardless of their creditworthiness.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.

meta_description: Chevron — NCUA-insured credit union in Concord, CA. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
